discriminators: A designed/processed vocal: pitch-shifted, formant-warped or layered, so it can land anywhere in feature space. No DSP prior can own it.
Tends to be more inharmonic and higher-flux than a natural human vocal, but that is a tendency only — the label is authorial intent, not acoustics.
Highest fundamental in the human taxonomy: infant cry f0 typically 400–600 Hz, babble and coos somewhat lower. Pitch above ~350 Hz with strong voicing is the core cue.
Non-lexical: babble lacks the regular ~4 Hz syllabic train of adult speech, so syllabic_modulation_energy sits below VOICES-MALE/FEMALE.
f0 distributions genuinely overlap VOICES-CHILD at the boundary — this narrows, it does not decide. Prefer text agreement.

explanation: A single person cheering. For a group use CROWDS-CHEERING.

discriminators: UCS rule: a SINGLE person cheering; the group is CROWDS-CHEERING. The signal cue for 'single' is a clean single-f0 track with a narrow stereo image, not the smeared multi-talker bed of a crowd.
Loud and voiced but shorter and less strained than VOICES-SCREAM, which shows rough phonation (high inharmonicity) and a higher f0.
Contains no dense unvoiced impulse train, which is what distinguishes it from CROWDS-APPLAUSE.

explanation: Various vocals for a child or a teenager. For non-discernible vocals for a very young child use VOICES-BABY.

discriminators: Pre-pubescent and teen f0 ≈ 250–400 Hz — above adult female, below infant cry — carried on the ~4 Hz syllabic modulation of real speech.
UCS rule: non-discernible vocals from a very young child go to VOICES-BABY. The presence of a lexical syllabic train is the cue that favours CHILD.
f0 overlaps VOICES-FEMALE at the top of the female range and VOICES-BABY at the bottom of the infant range; treat as a shortlist, not a verdict.
Voiced wailing: a strongly modulated pitch contour (large slow f0 excursions) with sobbing breath-catches, giving high brightness and pitch deviation over a long-ish take.
Less brief and less consistently loud than VOICES-SCREAM, and not the rhythmic pulse train of VOICES-LAUGH, which pulses at ~4–6 /s.
Whimpering and sniffling drag voicing_ratio down toward HUMAN-SNIFF; the wailing pitch contour is the tiebreak.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

explanation: A single fight and vocal efforts
and things like that. Boxing match vocals

discriminators: Short single grunts, exertions and strained exhales: voiced but non-lexical, so voicing is present while the ~4 Hz syllabic train is weak or absent — the opposite of VOICES-MALE dialog.
Rougher (higher inharmonicity) and much shorter than VOICES-SINGING; lower and less sustained than VOICES-SCREAM.
Heavy breathing components blur into HUMAN-BREATH; the presence of voicing is the tiebreak.

explanation: Any generic female vocal or dialog or misc vocal that doesn't fit somewhere else.

discriminators: Adult female f0 ≈ 165–255 Hz with the canonical ~4 Hz syllabic modulation of connected speech — the strongest 'this is speech' cue available anywhere in audio.
The male and female f0 distributions genuinely overlap (a low alto against a high tenor), so this narrows the field and must not be asserted alone; require text agreement.
Above VOICES-MALE and below VOICES-CHILD on pitch; full-bandwidth and undistorted, unlike VOICES-FUTZED.

A PROCESSING label, not a source: speech that has been band-limited and distorted. The telephone/PA band (roughly 300 Hz–3.4 kHz) is directly visible as a hard band_limit_high_hz with a hollowed low end and near-zero energy above 4 kHz. Catch it confidently.
It is still speech — voicing and ~4 Hz syllabic modulation stay high — so the band limit plus THD, not the voice itself, is what selects it over VOICES-MALE/FEMALE.
Distinguish from VOICES-HISTORICAL: archival recordings are also band-limited, but they roll off gradually with a high noise floor rather than showing a clean modern LP-and-distortion chain.
Defined by provenance — 'famous speeches, famous news broadcasts, presidential speeches' — not by sound. Nothing in the signal says a speech is famous. Excluded from signal scoring; text must drive.
The only honest signal correlate is the era of the medium: reduced bandwidth (often a 4–6 kHz ceiling), a raised noise floor and mild distortion. A weak hint, never a decision.
If the band limit is a hard, modern ~3.4 kHz telephone/PA notch with clean distortion, prefer VOICES-FUTZED.

The one genuinely measurable vocal rhythm: a periodic train of voiced 'ha' pulses at roughly 4–6 /s. High onset_rate AND high voicing_ratio together is the signature — nothing else in the taxonomy has both.
CROWDS-APPLAUSE has the onset train but no voicing; VOICES-MALE/FEMALE have the voicing but no impulsive pulse train.
Single voice: a narrow stereo image and one coherent f0 track, unlike the smeared multi-talker CROWDS-LAUGHTER, whose per-laugher periodicity averages away.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

discriminators: Adult male f0 ≈ 85–155 Hz, with the ~4 Hz syllabic modulation that marks connected speech; a lower spectral centroid and a bigger sub-200 Hz share than VOICES-FEMALE.
The male and female f0 distributions overlap in reality — this is a shortlist, not a verdict. Require text agreement before committing.
Full-bandwidth: a hard ~3.4 kHz ceiling means VOICES-FUTZED instead, whatever the talker.

explanation: The Voice category is for single voices. Groups of people go into the CROWDS categories.

discriminators: The category's abstain target. UCS rule: VOICES is for SINGLE voices — groups belong to CROWDS. A narrow stereo image and a single coherent f0 track are the 'single voice' evidence; a smeared multi-talker bed should route to CROWDS instead.
Assert only when the file is clearly one human voice but no sibling morphology (laugh, scream, whisper, cry, song) wins.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

explanation: A single Ooo or Aah and things of that nature.

discriminators: A single sustained interjection — 'ooo', 'aah', 'hmm'. Held and voiced: high sustain_ratio with LOW 3–8 Hz syllabic modulation, because there is only one syllable. That voiced-but-unsyllabic pairing is the discriminator against VOICES-MALE/FEMALE dialog.
Quieter and less strained than VOICES-CHEER; not the rhythmic pulse train of VOICES-LAUGH.
Single voice: the group version is CROWDS-REACTION, which is wide, dense and multi-f0.

Three cues fire together and nothing else in the taxonomy has all three: a very high f0 (typically 400–1000 Hz, well above the talker's speaking range), a very high absolute level, and ROUGH phonation — inharmonicity and noise inside the harmonic structure from vocal-fold chaos.
Rougher and louder than VOICES-SINGING at the same pitch, and far louder and higher than VOICES-EFFORTS.
Single voice; the group is CROWDS-PANIC or CROWDS-BATTLE, which are wide and multi-f0.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

explanation: a child singing. For a crowd

discriminators: Sung voice holds pitch: far higher voicing_ratio, harmonicity and sustain_ratio than spoken VOICES-MALE/FEMALE, and a much LOWER 3–8 Hz syllabic modulation because notes are held rather than delivered at syllable rate.
Clean harmonic phonation — the opposite of VOICES-SCREAM's rough, inharmonic strain, even at the same f0.
Single voice: for a group use CROWDS-SINGING, which is wide and multi-f0 with chorus-smeared partials.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)

explanation: A single whispering voice. The crowd equivalent would be CROWDS-QUIET.

discriminators: One of the cleanest cases in the whole taxonomy: whispering is UNVOICED speech. The vocal folds never engage, so voicing_ratio collapses to ~0 and harmonicity with it, spectral_flatness goes high and the level goes very low — yet the 3–8 Hz syllabic modulation of speech SURVIVES.

That combination — speech-rate modulation with zero voicing — belongs to nothing else. It separates WHISPER from every other VOICES subcategory (all voiced) and from HUMAN-BREATH (also unvoiced, but with no syllabic structure).
Single voice; the crowd equivalent is CROWDS-QUIET per the UCS explanation — but a murmuring crowd is still voiced, so that is a different test.

measured_evidence.selection: clips whose FSD50K labels map unambiguously to this one subcategory (high/medium crosswalk confidence only)
